intermediate.title: B1 First conditional, future time clauses
intermediate.content:
📌 First Conditional – Điều kiện có thể xảy ra
Cấu trúc: If + Present Simple, will + base verb
If it rains, I will stay at home.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.

• Dùng để nói về những tình huống có thể xảy ra trong tương lai.
• Mệnh đề If dùng Present Simple (KHÔNG dùng will sau if).
• Hai mệnh đề có thể đổi chỗ:
I will stay at home if it rains.

✦ Ngoài will, ta có thể dùng: might, can, should, be going to
If you're hungry, you can have a sandwich.
If it rains, we might cancel the match.
📌 Future Time Clauses – Mệnh đề chỉ thời gian trong tương lai
Sau when, as soon as, before, after, until, by the time + mệnh đề tương lai → dùng Present Simple (KHÔNG dùng will):

I'll call you when I arrive. (NOT when I will arrive)
As soon as she gets home, we'll have dinner.
He won't leave until you say sorry.
Before you go, please turn off the lights.

⚠️ So sánh if và when:
• If = có thể xảy ra, có thể không: If I see her, I'll tell her.
• When = chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra: When I see her, I'll tell her.
⚠️ Lỗi phổ biến
❌ If it will rain, I will stay home.
✅ If it rains, I will stay home.

❌ I'll call you when I will arrive.
✅ I'll call you when I arrive.

❌ If I won't pass, I'll be upset.
✅ If I don't pass, I'll be upset.
If you miss the train, you will be late for the meeting.If + Present Simple, will + base verb
I won't leave until you tell me the truth.until + Present Simple trong mệnh đề thời gian tương lai

exercises.q: If she ___ hard, she will pass the exam.
We ___ the trip if the weather is bad.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: main clause → will + base verb

exercises.q: I'll call you as soon as I ___ the station.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (as soon as) → Present Simple, không dùng will

exercises.q: If you ___ me, I will never forgive you.
exercises.opts: lie to,will lie to,lied to,are lying to

exercises.q: She won't leave until he ___ the truth.
If it ___ tomorrow, we can have a picnic.
exercises.exp: Negative First Conditional: if + doesn't + verb

exercises.q: When she ___ home, I'll tell her the news.
If you are hungry, you ___ have some of my pasta.
He ___ the job if he arrives late for the interview.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: main clause → will + base verb

exercises.q: Before you ___, please lock the door.
If she ___ to go, nobody can stop her.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: if + Present Simple (stative verb)

exercises.q: I'll send you the file as soon as I ___ it.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (as soon as) → Present Simple

exercises.q: If we don't hurry, we ___ the last bus.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: main clause → will + base verb

exercises.q: By the time you ___, the film will have started.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (by the time) → Present Simple

exercises.q: If you ___ me how to do it, I'll help you.
She ___ very happy if she gets the scholarship.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: main clause → will be

exercises.q: After he ___ the report, he'll send it to his manager.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (after) → Present Simple or Present Perfect

exercises.q: If I ___ my homework early, I might go out tonight.
Wait here until I ___ back.
You ___ be surprised if you see how much she has improved.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: will in main clause

exercises.q: If she ___ (not/hurry), she will miss the meeting.
exercises.exp: First Conditional negative: if + doesn't + verb

exercises.q: I will call you as soon as I ___ (arrive) at the airport.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (as soon as) → Present Simple

exercises.q: If you ___ (eat) too much, you will feel ill.
She ___ (not/leave) until she speaks to the manager.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (until) → won't in main clause

exercises.q: If the package ___ (arrive) today, please sign for it.
He ___ (take) the job if the salary is good enough.
exercises.exp: First Conditional: main clause → will + base verb

exercises.q: When she ___ (get) the news, she will be shocked.
If it ___ (be) sunny this weekend, we'll have a barbecue.
Before you ___ (go) to bed, don't forget to set your alarm.
If you ___ (practise) regularly, your English will improve quickly.
By the time the guests ___ (arrive), I will have prepared everything.
exercises.exp: Future time clause (by the time) → Present Simple

exercises.q: If the traffic ___ (be) bad, we might be late.
She will tell you the answer after she ___ (check) her notes.
If you ___ (not/water) the plants, they will die.
I ___ (wait) here until you come back.
exercises.exp: Main clause + until future time clause

exercises.q: As soon as the results ___ (come out), I will let you know immediately.
exercises.exp: Future time clause: as soon as + present simple

exercises.q: If you ___ (not/save) your work regularly, you might lose everything.
exercises.exp: First conditional negative: if + don't + verb

exercises.q: She won't attend the meeting unless she ___ (finish) the report first.
By the time we ___ (arrive), the concert will probably have started.
exercises.exp: Future time clause: by the time + present simple

exercises.q: If the traffic ___ (be) bad tomorrow, we should leave an hour earlier.

reorder.words: If she studies hard she will pass the exam
I will call you as soon as I reach the station
We will cancel the trip if the weather is bad
She won't leave until he tells her the truth
If it doesn't rain tomorrow we can have a picnic
When she gets home I will tell her the good news
He won't get the job if he arrives late for the interview
Before you leave please lock the door behind you
If we don't hurry we will miss the last bus
By the time you arrive the film will have already started
If you show me how to do it I will help you
She will be very happy if she gets the scholarship
After he finishes the report he will send it to his manager
Wait here until I come back from the shop
If you practise regularly your English will improve very quickly
If it is sunny this weekend we will have a barbecue
She will tell you the answer after she checks her notes
If you do not water the plants they will die very quickly
If the traffic is bad we might be late for the concert
I will send you the file as soon as I finish it

passage.text: Hi Sam! I'm writing to tell you about our plan for next week. If the weather {{1}} nice, we {{2}} a picnic in the botanical garden. I {{3}} some food as soon as I {{4}} shopping on Saturday. When everyone {{5}}, we {{6}} near the big fountain. If it {{7}}, we {{8}} the park café instead. After we {{9}} the picnic, we might go for a walk along the river. I'll send you the exact address before you {{10}}. If you {{11}} the address clearly, just call me. I {{12}} you the moment I {{13}} up that morning. See you soon!

Dear customers, if you {{1}} any problems with your order, please contact us immediately. We {{2}} you a full refund if the product {{3}} damaged on arrival. As soon as we {{4}} your complaint, a member of our team {{5}} you within 24 hours. If the item {{6}} in stock, we {{7}} a replacement right away. Before you {{8}} the package, please take photos of the damage. If you {{9}} any further assistance, you {{10}} our helpline at any time. When our technician {{11}} you, please have your order number ready. We {{12}} sure everything {{13}} sorted as quickly as possible.

Every spring, our school organises an outdoor science fair. If students {{1}} a project, they {{2}} in the main competition. When they {{3}} their projects, judges {{4}} them carefully. If a student {{5}} first place, they {{6}} a scholarship to attend a summer science camp. As soon as the winners {{7}}, the results {{8}} on the school website. Before the event {{9}}, teachers {{10}} students prepare their presentations. If a student {{11}} nervous, they {{12}} practise in front of their class first. The fair {{13}} unless the school {{14}} a very serious problem.

My friend Ana is planning her gap year. If she {{1}} enough money, she {{2}} to South America first. She {{3}} her trip as soon as she {{4}} her university exams in June. When she {{5}} a city, she {{6}} with local families to save money. If she {{7}} a job abroad, she {{8}} to stay longer. She {{9}} her family every week until she {{10}} back in September. If she {{11}} internet access, she {{12}} a travel blog. Before she {{13}}, she plans to visit at least fifteen countries. If the experience {{14}} her expectations, she might consider working abroad permanently.

Technology is changing how we work. If more companies {{1}} flexible hours, workers {{2}} a much better work-life balance. When employees {{3}} from home, they {{4}} time and money on commuting. If a business {{5}} good technology tools, its team {{6}} more efficiently. As soon as a company {{7}} hybrid working, staff satisfaction usually {{8}}. If managers {{9}} their teams regularly, performance {{10}} consistently. Before a new system {{11}}, companies must train their employees carefully. If workers {{12}} comfortable with digital tools, productivity {{13}} significantly.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về kế hoạch cho kỳ nghỉ sắp tới của bạn – điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu thời tiết tốt, điều gì bạn sẽ làm khi đến nơi và điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu kế hoạch thay đổi
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional and future time clauses: if, when, as soon as, before, until
writing_prompts.example: Next month, I am planning a trip to Da Lat with my family. If the weather is good, we will spend most of our time outdoors hiking and visiting flower gardens. As soon as we arrive, we will check in to the hotel and then explore the area near the lake. If we have time in the evening, we might visit the night market. Before we leave, we will make a list of all the restaurants we want to try. If it rains, we will visit the local museums instead. When we get back to Ho Chi Minh City, I will write a detailed travel blog about everything we experienced.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về mục tiêu học tập hoặc nghề nghiệp – những điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu bạn đạt được mục tiêu, bạn sẽ làm gì khi tốt nghiệp và bạn sẽ làm gì nếu gặp khó khăn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional for future goals and contingencies
writing_prompts.example: I am currently preparing for my final university exams and thinking about my future. If I pass all my exams, I will apply for a position at a marketing company in Ho Chi Minh City. When I graduate, I will update my CV and start sending applications immediately. As soon as I get an interview invitation, I will prepare thoroughly and research the company carefully. If I don't get the first job I apply for, I won't give up. I will keep trying until I find the right opportunity. Before I make any final decisions about my career, I plan to speak to professionals in the field for advice.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một vấn đề môi trường – điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu mọi người không thay đổi thói quen và điều gì sẽ cải thiện nếu họ hành động ngay
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional for environmental cause and effect
writing_prompts.example: Climate change is one of the most urgent problems we face today. If people don't reduce their carbon footprint soon, global temperatures will continue to rise dangerously. If we don't protect our forests, many species will disappear forever. However, there is still hope. If everyone switches to renewable energy, pollution will decrease significantly. As soon as governments introduce stricter environmental laws, companies will be forced to change their practices. If more people use public transport and electric vehicles, air quality in cities will improve considerably. Before it is too late, we must act together because if we wait, the damage will be much harder to reverse.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về kế hoạch làm việc hoặc kinh doanh – điều gì sẽ xảy ra khi bạn bắt đầu, điều gì sẽ thay đổi nếu thành công và những gì bạn sẽ làm nếu gặp trở ngại
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional for business planning scenarios
writing_prompts.example: My friend and I are planning to open a small café in the city centre next year. Before we start, we will write a detailed business plan and research the local market carefully. As soon as we find the right location, we will begin the renovation work. When we open, we will offer a special discount to all customers during the first month. If the café becomes popular, we will expand the menu and hire more staff. If we have any financial difficulties, we will apply for a small business loan. I am confident that if we work hard and listen to our customers, the business will be a success.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một cuộc gặp gỡ hay sự kiện sắp tới – bạn sẽ làm gì khi gặp ai đó, nói gì khi có cơ hội và chuẩn bị ra sao trước khi đến
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional and future time clauses in social context
writing_prompts.example: Next Friday, I am attending my university reunion and I am quite excited. Before I go, I will prepare a few conversation topics so I don't feel awkward. When I arrive, I will look for my closest friends first. As soon as I see my old roommate Minh, I will give him the book I borrowed two years ago. If anyone asks about my job, I will tell them about my recent promotion. I won't leave until I have spoken to everyone I want to catch up with. If there is time after the dinner, we might all go for a walk by the lake. I'm sure that if the weather is nice, it will be a perfect evening.

intermediate.title: B1 Second conditional: Unreal situations
intermediate.content:
📌 Second Conditional – Tình huống giả định / không thực
Cấu trúc: If + Past Simple, would + base verb
If I had more money, I would travel the world.
If she knew the answer, she would tell you.

• Dùng để nói về tình huống không thực ở hiện tại hoặc tương lai (giả định, ước mơ, lời khuyên).
• Hàm ý: thực tế là ngược lại.
If I were you... (thực tế: I am not you)
If I had time... (thực tế: I don't have time)
📌 Were thay cho was trong điều kiện loại 2
Trong văn phong trang trọng, dùng were cho tất cả ngôi (kể cả I/he/she):
If I were you, I wouldn't do that.
If she were here, she would know what to do.

Trong văn nói/không trang trọng, was cũng được chấp nhận:
If I was richer, I would buy a bigger house.
📌 Thay thế would bằng could / might
If I had wings, I could fly. (khả năng)
If it were warmer, I might go for a swim. (có thể)
⚠️ Lỗi phổ biến
❌ If I would have more money, I would travel.
✅ If I had more money, I would travel.

❌ If she would be here, she would help.
✅ If she were here, she would help.
If I were taller, I would play basketball professionally.Giả định không thực ở hiện tại
What would you do if you won the lottery?Câu hỏi giả định – won là Past Simple (không phải thực tế)

exercises.q: If I ___ more free time, I would take up a new hobby.
She would call you if she ___ your number.
If I were you, I ___ accept that job offer.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: main clause → would + base verb

exercises.q: What would you do if you ___ a million dollars?
If she ___ here, she would know what to do.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: if + were (formal) for all subjects

exercises.q: He ___ a bike if he lived closer to work.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: main clause → would + base verb

exercises.q: If I ___ the CEO, I would change many things.
She could travel more if she ___ less busy.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional with could; if + were

exercises.q: If they ___ harder, they wouldn't always fall behind.
I would be so happy if she ___ to visit us this winter.
If we ___ in a bigger city, we would have more opportunities.
He might exercise more if he ___ a gym near his house.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional with might; if + Past Simple

exercises.q: If I ___ speak fluent Spanish, I would apply for that position.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: if + could (ability in hypothetical)

exercises.q: What would you do if your boss ___ you to work overtime?
She wouldn't be so stressed if she ___ a different job.
If animals ___ speak, what do you think they would say?
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: if + could (ability in hypothetical)

exercises.q: I would be more productive if I ___ better.
If the tickets ___ cheaper, more people would go.
She would volunteer abroad if she ___ the language.
If I ___ the chance, I would study medicine.
If I ___ (be) you, I would apologise immediately.
She would travel more if she ___ (not/be) so afraid of flying.
If he ___ (work) harder, he would earn more money.
I ___ (buy) a house in the countryside if I could afford it.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: main clause → would + base verb

exercises.q: What ___ you ___ (do) if you had a whole year free?
exercises.exp: Second Conditional question: what + would + subject + verb

exercises.q: They would come to the party if they ___ (not/have) to work.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional negative: if + didn't have

exercises.q: If the weather ___ (be) warmer, we could eat outside.
She ___ (not/feel) so tired if she went to bed earlier.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: main clause → wouldn't + base verb

exercises.q: If I ___ (live) near the beach, I would swim every day.
He could do a better job if he ___ (receive) more training.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional with could: if + Past Simple

exercises.q: If animals ___ (can) talk, life would be much more interesting.
I would read more books if I ___ (not/watch) so much TV.
If the government ___ (reduce) taxes, more businesses would open.
She might feel better if she ___ (talk) to someone about her problems.
If I ___ (have) superpowers, I would help people in need.
If I ___ (be) taller, I would consider a career in basketball.
She would travel the world if she ___ (not/have) so many responsibilities.
exercises.exp: Second conditional negative: if + didn't + verb

exercises.q: If he ___ (know) her number, he would call her right away.
exercises.exp: Second conditional: if + past simple (unreal present)

exercises.q: What ___ you ___ (do) if you found a large sum of money on the street?
If we ___ (live) closer to the sea, we would go swimming every weekend.

reorder.words: If I had more free time I would take up a new hobby
She would call you if she knew your phone number
If I were you I would not accept that job offer
What would you do if you won one million dollars
If she were here she would know exactly what to do
He would buy a bike if he lived closer to work
If the tickets were cheaper more people would go to the concert
She could travel more if she were not so afraid of flying
I would be more productive if I slept better at night
If we lived in a bigger city we would have more job opportunities
What would you do if your boss asked you to work overtime
If I were the president I would make education completely free
They would come to the party if they did not have to work
If animals could talk life would be much more interesting
I would read more books if I did not watch so much television
She would not be so stressed if she had a different job
If he worked harder he would earn a lot more money
I would buy a house in the countryside if I could afford it
She might feel better if she talked to someone about her feelings
If I lived near the beach I would go swimming every single day

passage.text: My friend Linh loves talking about what she {{1}} if she {{2}} a superhero. She says that if she {{3}} the power to fly, she {{4}} everywhere without using any transport. If she {{5}} invisible, she {{6}} to the most important meetings in the world and listen in. She {{7}} world peace if she {{8}} the ability to speak all languages fluently. If she {{9}} a superhero team, she {{10}} the most talented people in the world. She also says that if she {{11}} very powerful, she {{12}} careful not to misuse her powers. Finally, she says that if she {{13}} one wish, she {{14}} that all children in the world had access to good education.

Imagine a world where cars {{1}} exist. Life {{2}} very different from what we know today. If people {{3}} cars, they {{4}} to walk, cycle, or use public transport everywhere. Cities {{5}} much less polluted if there {{6}} no car exhaust. Children {{7}} more freely in the streets if it {{8}} safe. The air {{9}} cleaner and people {{10}} healthier. If the world {{11}} this way, we {{12}} less oil and the environment {{13}} in much better condition. Of course, some things {{14}} more difficult, but overall, life might actually be better.

Every year our school runs a 'What if?' creative writing competition. This year the theme was: what {{1}} your life look like if you {{2}} one special talent? One student wrote that if she {{3}} the ability to speak every language, she {{4}} as an international diplomat and {{5}} to solve conflicts around the world. Another imagined that if he {{6}} a gifted musician, he {{7}} free concerts in hospitals and schools. A third student said that if she {{8}} time, she {{9}} go back and fix her biggest mistakes. One boy wrote that if he {{10}} invisible, he {{11}} meetings at the United Nations. The judges said they {{12}} the results more interesting if students {{13}} such creative interpretations.

My colleague Rafa often talks about what he {{1}} differently if he {{2}} his life again. He says that if he {{3}} the choice, he {{4}} medicine instead of law. He believes he {{5}} a better doctor than a lawyer. He also says that if he {{6}} more time, he {{7}} a language — probably Japanese. Sometimes he wonders what {{8}} if he {{9}} that job offer in Tokyo five years ago. He thinks he {{10}} happier now if he {{11}} that opportunity. I always tell him it {{12}} no use imagining the past. But he says that if he {{13}} all his choices again, at least one thing {{14}} the same — he {{15}} the same group of friends.

Last week I attended a talk about sustainable living. The speaker asked: 'What {{1}} you change first if you {{2}} more control over your daily habits?' One woman said she {{3}} a bicycle to work if she {{4}} closer to the office. A man said he {{5}} less meat if healthy plant-based options {{6}} cheaper. A teenager said that if she {{7}} her own home, she {{8}} solar panels immediately. Someone else said that if governments {{9}} better public transport, more people {{10}} their cars less. The speaker smiled and said: 'If everyone in this room {{11}} just one thing, the impact {{12}} enormous. And if we {{13}} now, we {{14}} a real difference for future generations.'

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về điều bạn sẽ làm nếu bạn thắng một khoản tiền lớn – bạn sẽ chi tiêu như thế nào, giúp đỡ ai và cuộc sống bạn sẽ thay đổi như thế nào
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Second conditional: if I won, I would, I could, I might
writing_prompts.example: If I won a large sum of money in the lottery, my life would change dramatically. First, I would make sure my family was comfortable by buying my parents a new home. I would also pay off any debts so that I could have complete financial freedom. If I had enough money, I might start my own business in sustainable food production. I would donate a significant portion to charities that support education for children in remote areas. I would travel to places I have always dreamed of, like Iceland and Patagonia. However, I would be careful not to spend everything at once. If I were very wealthy, I would still want to work because I believe working gives life purpose and meaning.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một vấn đề xã hội và điều gì sẽ cải thiện nếu mọi người hoặc chính phủ hành động khác đi
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Second conditional for social commentary: if people/the government + past simple, would + base verb
writing_prompts.example: Traffic congestion is a serious problem in Ho Chi Minh City. If the government invested more in public transport, fewer people would use private motorbikes and cars. If the metro system were expanded to more areas, commuting would become much easier for millions of people. If more people cycled to work, air pollution would decrease significantly and the streets would be safer. If companies allowed flexible working hours, fewer vehicles would be on the road at peak times. Traffic education would also help. If drivers followed the rules more carefully, accidents would be less frequent. I believe that if all these changes were made together, life in our city would be much more pleasant for everyone.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy tưởng tượng bạn là một người nổi tiếng hoặc nhân vật lịch sử – cuộc sống của bạn sẽ như thế nào và bạn sẽ làm gì khác đi
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Second conditional for hypothetical identity: if I were, I would, could
writing_prompts.example: If I were a famous scientist like Marie Curie, I would dedicate my life to finding solutions to the world's most pressing health problems. I would set up research institutes where young scientists could work with the best equipment. If I were that influential, I would use my platform to encourage more girls to study science and pursue careers in research. I might feel a great sense of responsibility to make discoveries that truly benefited humanity. I would also make sure that my findings were shared freely with researchers around the world. However, I would probably find the lack of privacy difficult. If I were famous, I wouldn't have much time for my personal life, and that would be a real sacrifice.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về cuộc sống lý tưởng của bạn – bạn sẽ sống ở đâu, làm nghề gì, và cuộc sống hàng ngày của bạn sẽ trông như thế nào nếu mọi thứ hoàn hảo
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Second conditional for ideal life scenario
writing_prompts.example: If I could design my ideal life, it would look quite different from my current situation. I would live in a small cottage near the sea with a large garden where I could grow vegetables and flowers. I would work as a writer and illustrator, creating books for children. I wouldn't have to commute because I would work from home and set my own hours. I would wake up early every morning to walk along the beach before sitting down to work. I could spend my evenings reading, cooking, or playing music. If my life were this way, I would feel much more relaxed and creative. I might travel to different countries every summer to gather inspiration for new stories. It sounds like a dream, but I intend to work towards it.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về việc thay đổi một quyết định trong quá khứ – nếu bạn có thể làm lại, bạn sẽ chọn khác không và tại sao
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Second conditional for current hypothetical about past: if I could go back, if I had the chance now
writing_prompts.example: If I could go back and make my university choices again, I would probably choose a different subject. I studied economics because it seemed practical, but if I had complete freedom to choose, I would study environmental science instead. If I could change my path now, I would retrain in that area because I am genuinely passionate about sustainability. I wouldn't waste any more time doing work that doesn't feel meaningful to me. If I had the chance to start over, I might also take a gap year before university to travel and gain life experience. I believe that if I were in that field, I would feel more motivated and happier in my daily work. It is never too late to make changes, and that is something I remind myself of regularly.

intermediate.title: B1 First and second conditionals
intermediate.content:
📌 So sánh First và Second Conditional
First ConditionalSecond Conditional
Cấu trúcIf + Present Simple, willIf + Past Simple, would
Ý nghĩaCó thể xảy ra thựcGiả định / không thực
Ví dụIf it rains, I will stay home.
(có thể trời mưa)		If it rained here, I would stay home.
(không thực – trời đang nắng)
Thực tếkhông biết kết quảthực tế ngược lại
📌 Ngữ cảnh quyết định loại conditional
If I see Tom, I will give him your message. → Có thể sẽ gặp Tom (1st)
If I saw Tom, I would give him your message. → Không chắc gặp / khó gặp (2nd)

If I have time, I will come. → Có thể có thời gian (1st)
If I had time, I would come. → Không có thời gian (2nd)
📌 Unless = if ... not
I won't go unless you come with me. = I won't go if you don't come with me.
Unless it stops raining, we'll have to cancel the match.

⚠️ Unless KHÔNG dùng với negative:
❌ Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late.
✅ Unless you hurry, you'll be late.
If I get a bonus this month, I will buy you dinner. (realistic – 1st)First: thực tế có thể xảy ra
If I got a bonus every month, I would save much more. (unreal – 2nd)Second: giả định – thực tế không phải vậy

exercises.q: If I ___ enough sleep tonight, I'll feel much better tomorrow.
exercises.exp: First Conditional – realistic possibility: if + Present Simple

exercises.q: If I ___ more sleep every night, I would feel so much better.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional – unreal/hypothetical: if + Past Simple

exercises.q: ___ you go to the concert if you had a free ticket?
exercises.exp: Second Conditional question: Would + subject + base verb

exercises.q: Unless you ___, you'll be late for the meeting.
exercises.exp: Unless = if not → positive verb form follows unless

exercises.q: If he ___ harder, his boss wouldn't be so disappointed.
If she ___ the exam, she will celebrate.
She wouldn't be so tired if she ___ to bed earlier.
I'll come to the party unless I ___ too much work.
exercises.exp: Unless = if not, followed by positive verb

exercises.q: If we ___ a garden, we would grow our own vegetables.
If the train ___ on time, we'll make our connection.
He ___ help you if he were in town.
Unless she ___ her phone, she won't hear the alarm.
exercises.exp: Unless + positive verb = if she doesn't charge

exercises.q: If I see Maria at the party, I ___ her your message.
If I saw Maria today, I ___ her your message.
She ___ the project on time if she had more help.
If it ___ so cold, we could have lunch outside.
exercises.exp: Second Conditional: if + weren't (unreal – it IS cold)

exercises.q: We'll go to the beach if the weather ___.
If you ___ me earlier, I could have helped you.
If I ___ the lottery, I would quit my job immediately.
Unless you ___ the form today, your application will be rejected.
exercises.exp: Unless + positive verb (submit) = if you don't submit

exercises.q: If she ___ (study) more, she will definitely pass the exam.
If she ___ (study) more, she would definitely pass the exam.
___ (you/accept) the job offer if they paid you more?
Unless it ___ (stop) raining, we will have to cancel the match.
If I ___ (be) you, I would think more carefully before deciding.
I ___ (help) you if I had more time, but I'm very busy.
If the weather ___ (be) good this Saturday, we'll go hiking.
She won't come unless you ___ (invite) her personally.
He ___ (not/be) so stressed if he exercised more regularly.
If the price ___ (drop), more people will buy the product.
If you ___ (not/book) early, all the seats will be taken.
I ___ (take) a taxi if I missed the last bus.
Unless he ___ (apologise), she won't forgive him.
If they ___ (offer) me the role, I will probably accept.
What ___ you ___ (do) if you were invisible for a day?
If the price ___ (drop) next month, I will definitely buy the new model.
If I ___ (be) you, I wouldn't accept that offer without negotiating first.
exercises.exp: Second conditional (advice): If I were you…

exercises.q: Unless it ___ (stop) raining soon, the outdoor ceremony will be moved inside.
exercises.exp: First conditional with unless: unless + present simple

exercises.q: She ___ (feel) much better if she took a proper break from work.
If he calls while I'm out, tell him I ___ (ring) him back this evening.
exercises.exp: First conditional: main clause → will + verb

reorder.words: If she studies harder she will definitely pass the exam
If she studied harder she would definitely pass the exam
Unless you hurry you will be late for the important meeting
Would you go to the concert if you had a free ticket
If I were you I would think carefully before making a decision
I will come to the party unless I have too much work
If the train arrives on time we will make our connection
He would help you if he were in town this week
If I see Maria at the party I will give her your message
If I saw Maria today I would give her your message
Unless she charges her phone she won't hear the morning alarm
We will go to the beach if the weather improves this afternoon
If it were not so cold we could have lunch outside today
She would not be so tired if she went to bed earlier
If you do not book early all the seats will be taken
If he worked harder his boss would not be so disappointed
Unless you submit the form today your application will be rejected
She would not be so stressed if she exercised more regularly
If the price drops more people will buy the product online
If I won the lottery I would quit my job immediately

passage.text: My colleague Tom and I were discussing our careers over lunch. I told him that if I {{1}} a promotion this year, I {{2}} very relieved. He replied that if he {{3}} my position, he {{4}} to speak to the manager directly. Then he said something surprising. He told me that if he {{5}} the chance to change careers, he {{6}} something completely different. He said that unless he {{7}} more creative work soon, he {{8}} his current job. I asked him what he {{9}} if he {{10}} to start over. He said he {{11}} become a chef if he {{12}} enough money to go to cooking school. In the end, I reminded him that if he {{13}} nothing, his situation {{14}} the same.

Living in a foreign country can be both exciting and challenging. If you {{1}} abroad next year, you {{2}} many new experiences. However, if you {{3}} the language, things {{4}} much easier. Unless you {{5}} at least some basic phrases, you {{6}} difficulties with simple everyday tasks. If you {{7}} a local, you {{8}} a much better understanding of the culture. On the other hand, if you {{9}} homesick, you might {{10}} going home early. If I {{11}} in your position, I {{12}} as much as possible before leaving. But ultimately, if you {{13}} the opportunity, you {{14}} it. People who have lived abroad say it changes them for life.

My friend Duy is deciding whether to move abroad for work. He says: 'If I {{1}} the offer, I will move to Singapore next spring. But if I {{2}} — which sometimes seems tempting — I {{3}} in my current job and save money here instead.' If he {{4}}, he {{5}} a much higher salary but {{6}} far from his family. If he {{7}}, things {{8}} the same and he {{9}} his parents every weekend. He says that if a recruiter {{10}} him a role closer to home, he {{11}} it without hesitation. He {{12}} a final decision as soon as he {{13}} back from the company. One thing is certain: if he {{14}} abroad, his whole life {{15}}.

Our company is planning a new product launch. The manager explained the two scenarios at the meeting. She said: 'If the market research {{1}} positive results next month, we {{2}} the launch in April. However, if there {{3}} significant problems with the data, we {{4}} the timeline.' She also said that if the budget {{5}} approved this week, the team {{6}} immediately. But if the board {{7}} further changes, everything {{8}} by another month. One team member asked: 'What {{9}} if a competitor {{10}} a similar product first?' The manager replied: 'If that {{11}}, we {{12}} our strategy and {{13}} on our unique features. If I {{14}} the board, I {{15}} a more flexible launch plan from the start.'

My grandmother loves giving advice. She always says: 'If you {{1}} hard enough, you will achieve anything you set your mind to.' She also believes that if young people {{2}} more time outdoors, they {{3}} happier and healthier. She tells me: 'If I {{4}} your age again, I {{5}} every moment instead of worrying so much.' She often asks: 'What {{6}} you do differently if you {{7}} a second chance?' I always say that if I {{8}} the chance, I {{9}} music when I was young. She smiles and says: 'It {{10}} too late. If you {{11}} now, you will surprise yourself.' She adds: 'And if you ever {{12}} my advice, I will always be here to listen.'

writing_prompts.vi: So sánh hai tình huống: điều thực tế có thể xảy ra và điều bạn ước muốn – dùng cả First và Second Conditional trong cùng một bài viết
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional (realistic) vs Second conditional (hypothetical) in one text
writing_prompts.example: I am currently preparing for two possible futures. If I pass my final exams, I will apply for a master's degree programme in the UK. I have already researched several universities and I will submit my application as soon as my results come out. However, if I could choose my ideal path without any financial constraints, I would take a very different route. If money were no issue, I would spend two years travelling through Europe and Asia, learning languages and volunteering. I would visit historical sites and talk to people from completely different backgrounds. In reality, unless I receive a scholarship, studying abroad will be a big financial commitment. But I am excited about both possibilities and will work hard to make the most of whichever path opens up.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một vấn đề trong cộng đồng của bạn và đề xuất giải pháp – dùng First Conditional cho giải pháp thực tế và Second Conditional cho những gì lý tưởng hơn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mixed conditionals for problem-solving: real solutions (1st) and ideal solutions (2nd)
writing_prompts.example: Litter is a growing problem in my neighbourhood. If the local council installs more rubbish bins, people will be less likely to drop litter on the streets. If schools teach children about environmental responsibility, attitudes will gradually improve. Unless residents report illegal dumping, the situation will get worse. However, if I could design the ideal solution, I would completely redesign the waste collection system. If there were recycling stations on every corner, people would find it easier to separate their waste. If the city offered financial rewards for recycling, more people would participate. I believe that if everyone played their part, our streets would be much cleaner within a year.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một người bạn hoặc người thân đang đứng trước quyết định – đưa ra lời khuyên dùng First và Second Conditional
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Advice using conditionals: if you do X, you will; if I were you, I would
writing_prompts.example: My friend Nam has been offered a job in Singapore but is worried about leaving his family. I told him that if he accepts the offer, he will gain incredible international experience that could transform his career. I also said that if he turns it down, he might always wonder what could have been. If I were in his position, I would seriously consider accepting it. If he negotiates with the company, he might be able to arrange regular trips back to Vietnam. Unless he tries living abroad, he will never know if he would enjoy it. I told him that if he plans carefully and stays in touch with his family, the transition will be manageable. I think it is a fantastic opportunity and I would take it without hesitation.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về cuộc sống trong tương lai gần (thực tế) và tương lai lý tưởng (giả định) – dùng cả hai loại câu điều kiện
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: First conditional for near-future plans, second conditional for dreams
writing_prompts.example: In the near future, I have some concrete plans. If I finish my language course this year, I will apply for jobs that require English skills. As soon as I save enough money, I will move to a bigger flat closer to work. If my company offers me a promotion, I will definitely take it. However, if I could design my ideal life without any limitations, things would look quite different. If I had the freedom to choose, I would open a small bookshop with a café where people could read and connect. If there were enough customers, I would host book clubs and literary events every week. I know this is a dream for now, but unless I work towards it, it will never happen. First conditional today, second conditional for tomorrow's dream.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về quyết định quan trọng bạn phải đưa ra sắp tới – điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu bạn chọn A, điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu bạn chọn B, và điều bạn thực sự muốn nếu không có ràng buộc
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both conditionals to weigh options
writing_prompts.example: I currently have two options for my future and I find it very hard to decide. If I choose to stay in my current job, I will have financial security and a predictable routine. Unless something unexpected happens, I will probably get promoted within two years. On the other hand, if I take the risk and start my own business, I might earn more eventually, but the first few years could be very difficult. If I were completely free of financial worries, I would definitely choose to be an entrepreneur. I would start a social enterprise focused on education access in rural areas. However, in reality, I have responsibilities. If I had no savings, starting a business would be extremely risky. I think if I save carefully for the next year, I will be in a much stronger position to make this decision.

intermediate.title: B1 Third conditional: Past unreal situations
intermediate.content:
📌 Third Conditional – Điều kiện trong quá khứ không có thực
Cấu trúc: If + Past Perfect, would have + past participle
If she had studied harder, she would have passed the exam.
If I had known about the party, I would have come.

• Dùng để nói về tình huống KHÔNG có thực trong quá khứ.
• Hàm ý: điều đó không xảy ra → kết quả cũng không xảy ra.

✦ Thực tế ẩn đằng sau:
→ If she had studied harder... = She didn't study hard (thực tế)
→ ...she would have passed... = She didn't pass (thực tế)
📌 Rút gọn và biến thể
Rút gọn:
I'd have known = I would have known
He'd have told = He would have told

Dùng could have / might have thay would have:
If we had left earlier, we could have caught the train. (khả năng)
If you had asked him, he might have helped. (có thể)
📌 Đảo mệnh đề
Có thể đảo vị trí hai mệnh đề, dùng dấu phẩy khi mệnh đề if đứng trước:
She would have passed if she had studied harder.
I would have come if I had known about the party.
⚠️ Lỗi phổ biến
❌ If I would have known, I would have told you.
✅ If I had known, I would have told you.

❌ If she had study harder, she would have passed.
✅ If she had studied harder, she would have passed.

❌ I would of gone.
✅ I would have gone. (have, NOT of)
If he had left earlier, he would have caught the bus.Third Conditional: hai sự kiện không có thực trong quá khứ
She might have won if she hadn't been so nervous.might have thay would have = khả năng

exercises.q: If she ___ harder, she would have passed the exam.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: if + Past Perfect (had studied)

exercises.q: He would have got the job if he ___ better at the interview.
If I had known about the meeting, I ___ there.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: They ___ the match if their best player hadn't been injured.
exercises.opts: might have won,would win,had won,could win

exercises.q: If we had booked earlier, the tickets ___ cheaper.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: She wouldn't have been late if she ___ her alarm.
If I had seen him, I ___ him your message.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: We ___ the last train if we hadn't stopped for coffee.
If he had taken my advice, he ___ those problems.
exercises.opts: wouldn't have had,wouldn't have,hadn't had,won't have
exercises.exp: Third Conditional negative: wouldn't have + PP

exercises.q: She ___ to the party if she had been invited.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: If they had known the truth, what ___ they ___?
exercises.exp: Third Conditional question: would + subject + have + PP

exercises.q: The accident ___ if the driver hadn't been distracted.
exercises.opts: wouldn't have happened,wouldn't happen,hadn't happened,won't happen
exercises.exp: Third Conditional negative: wouldn't have + PP

exercises.q: If he ___ the contract carefully, he wouldn't have signed it.
I ___ you if I had known you were sick.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: If the project ___ on time, the client would have been satisfied.
exercises.opts: had been completed,was completed,would be completed,has been completed
exercises.exp: Third Conditional passive: if + had been + PP

exercises.q: She might have become a doctor if she ___ medicine.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: might have + if + Past Perfect

exercises.q: If I ___ the directions correctly, I wouldn't have got lost.
They ___ so much money if they had planned more carefully.
exercises.opts: wouldn't have spent,wouldn't spend,hadn't spent,won't spend

exercises.q: If the rescue team ___ earlier, they could have saved more lives.
He ___ the presentation better if he had prepared more.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: If I ___ (know) you were coming, I would have prepared dinner.
She ___ (not/make) that mistake if she had checked her work.
If they ___ (leave) earlier, they would have caught the flight.
We ___ (win) the match if we had played better in the second half.
exercises.exp: Third Conditional: main clause → would have + PP

exercises.q: If the weather ___ (be) better, we would have gone camping.
He might ___ (become) a professional musician if he hadn't quit.
If I ___ (take) the other road, I would have avoided the traffic jam.
The patient ___ (survive) if the ambulance had arrived sooner.
She wouldn't have been upset if I ___ (not/say) that.
If he ___ (save) money earlier, he could have bought the house.
We ___ (not/be) so hungry if we had eaten breakfast.
If the instructions ___ (be) clearer, fewer people would have got confused.
She could ___ (finish) the project on time if she had started earlier.
What ___ you ___ (do) if you had found a large amount of money?
If the company ___ (invest) in training, productivity would have improved.
If she ___ (study) harder, she would have passed the exam with flying colours.
We ___ (not/be) late if the train hadn't been cancelled at the last minute.
exercises.exp: Third conditional negative: wouldn't have + past participle

exercises.q: If you ___ (tell) me sooner, I could have helped you with the problem.
He ___ (get) the job if he had prepared more thoroughly for the interview.
exercises.exp: Third conditional: would have + past participle

exercises.q: If they ___ (not/miss) the flight, they would have arrived on Tuesday.
exercises.exp: Third conditional negative: if + hadn't + past participle

reorder.words: If she had studied harder she would have passed the exam
He would have got the job if he had performed better at the interview
If I had known about the meeting I would have come
We could have caught the train if we had not stopped for coffee
If she had set her alarm she would not have been late
The accident would not have happened if the driver had been careful
If they had known the truth they would have acted differently
She might have won if she had not been so nervous
If he had taken my advice he would not have had those problems
I would have called you if I had known you were ill
If we had booked the tickets earlier they would have been cheaper
She would have gone to the party if she had been invited
They would not have spent so much money if they had planned carefully
If the rescue team had arrived earlier they could have saved more lives
If I had followed the directions I would not have got lost
He would have given a better presentation if he had prepared more
If they had left earlier they would have caught the last flight
The patient might have survived if the ambulance had arrived sooner
She might have become a doctor if she had studied medicine
If the instructions had been clearer fewer people would have been confused

passage.text: Last year, our school team entered a regional science competition. Looking back, I think we {{1}} if we {{2}} better. First, if our project leader {{3}} ill the week before the competition, she {{4}} the presentation properly. If she {{5}} enough time to practise, our delivery {{6}} much more confident. Secondly, if we {{7}} better research, the judges {{8}} more impressed with our data. Our design {{9}} a higher score if we {{10}} the prototype more carefully. However, even with all these issues, we {{11}} so many valuable skills. If we {{12}} this experience, we {{13}} how to handle pressure as a team. We are all grateful for the challenge.

The history of aviation {{1}} very different if the Wright Brothers {{2}} their famous first flight in 1903. If they {{3}} on that cold December morning, someone else {{4}} the credit. If aviation {{5}} as quickly as it did, millions of people {{6}} able to travel internationally by the mid-twentieth century. If early planes {{7}} safer, more passengers {{8}} to fly commercially sooner. Some historians argue that if certain wars {{9}}, aviation technology {{10}} as fast. The jet engine, for example, {{11}} developed so quickly if there {{12}} strong military demand for faster aircraft. It is fascinating to think how much {{13}} differently if any one of these key moments {{14}} differently.

My grandmother often tells me about decisions she made when she was young. She says that if she {{1}} her first job offer, her career {{2}} in a completely different direction. If she {{3}} that position, she {{4}} her future husband at the office. If they {{5}}, neither of my parents {{6}} born. She also says that if she {{7}} harder at school, she {{8}} to university. However, she always says she {{9}} her life differently even if she {{10}} the chance. If she {{11}} every challenge, she {{12}} the strong person she is today. Looking back, she says that even the difficult moments were important. If things {{13}} as they did, our whole family story {{14}} very different.

The great fire that destroyed a historic district of our town last summer {{1}} if someone {{2}} the gas pipe on time. If the alarm {{3}} sooner, firefighters {{4}} to the scene more quickly. If more water {{5}} available at the site, the fire {{6}} much faster. Several historic buildings {{7}} saved if the fire brigade {{8}} better equipment. Residents who lost their homes {{9}} much more support if the local government {{10}} better emergency plans. If journalists {{11}} the story more widely, more donations {{12}} in from around the country. People affected by the fire say that if they {{13}} warnings more seriously, they {{14}} more precious belongings.

Scientists believe that many health problems today {{1}} less common if societies {{2}} differently over the past century. If people {{3}} fewer processed foods, many diseases {{4}} so widespread. If the food industry {{5}} stricter regulations earlier, millions of lives {{6}} saved. If doctors {{7}} the importance of diet sooner, public health advice {{8}} more effective. Some researchers argue that if certain medical breakthroughs {{9}} earlier, treatments {{10}} more affordable. If the global community {{11}} more in preventive healthcare, hospitals {{12}} as overcrowded as they are today. These reflections remind us how important our choices are, both individually and collectively.

writing_prompts.vi: Nghĩ về một quyết định quan trọng trong quá khứ mà bạn ước giá như đã chọn khác – điều gì đã xảy ra và cuộc sống sẽ khác thế nào nếu bạn đã quyết định khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Third conditional: if I had, I would have; wouldn't have; could have
writing_prompts.example: When I finished secondary school, I chose to study economics because it seemed like a safe and practical option. Looking back, I sometimes wonder how different my life would have been if I had chosen to study design instead. If I had followed my passion for art and creativity, I would have pursued a completely different career path. I might have become a graphic designer or an architect. If I had been braver and taken that risk, I could have built a portfolio of creative work that I am truly proud of. However, I would not have met the colleagues and friends who have been so important to me over the years. If I hadn't studied economics, I wouldn't have developed the analytical and financial skills that help me so much today. I believe that even the decisions we regret teach us something valuable.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một sự kiện lịch sử hoặc khoa học nổi tiếng – điều gì sẽ khác đi nếu nó đã không xảy ra hoặc xảy ra theo cách khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Third conditional for historical hypotheticals
writing_prompts.example: The invention of the internet has transformed modern life in ways that are hard to fully measure. If Tim Berners-Lee had not developed the World Wide Web in 1989, global communication would have remained far more limited. If the internet had not become widely available in the 1990s, businesses would not have been able to operate internationally with such ease. Online education, streaming services, and social media would not have existed. If researchers had not shared information so freely through connected networks, many scientific breakthroughs might have taken decades longer to achieve. The COVID-19 pandemic would have been far more devastating if remote working and online learning had not been possible. It is remarkable to think how different every aspect of modern society would have been if this single technology had not been invented when it was.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy nhìn lại một sai lầm hoặc hiểu lầm mà bạn hoặc người khác đã mắc phải – điều gì đã dẫn đến sai lầm đó và kết quả sẽ khác như thế nào nếu ai đó đã hành động khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Third conditional to reflect on mistakes and consequences
writing_prompts.example: Last year, our class project ended in disaster because of a series of avoidable mistakes. If our team leader had communicated the deadlines clearly from the beginning, everyone would have known what to do. If we had checked in with each other more regularly, we would have noticed the problems much sooner. If one team member hadn't deleted the wrong files by accident, we could have submitted the full project on time. If only we had backed up our work on a shared drive, we wouldn't have lost three weeks of research. The teacher was understanding, but if she hadn't given us an extension, we would have failed the assignment entirely. It was a painful experience, but if we hadn't made those mistakes, we might not have learned how important teamwork and communication truly are.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một cơ hội bạn đã bỏ lỡ – tại sao bạn không nắm bắt nó và cuộc sống sẽ như thế nào nếu bạn đã làm khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Third conditional for missed opportunities: if I had taken, would have, could have
writing_prompts.example: Three years ago, I was offered the chance to join an international exchange programme and study in France for six months. At the time, I was afraid to go because I did not speak French well and I was worried about leaving my comfort zone. I turned it down, and I have sometimes regretted that decision. If I had taken that opportunity, I would have experienced an entirely different culture and improved my language skills dramatically. If I hadn't been so afraid of the unknown, I could have made lifelong friends from around the world. I might have discovered a passion for international work that could have shaped my entire career. If only I had been braver, my life today might look very different. However, I have learnt from this experience and I now try to say yes to new opportunities whenever they appear.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một tình huống trong câu chuyện sách/phim yêu thích của bạn – điều gì sẽ xảy ra khác nếu một nhân vật đã hành động khác đi
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Third conditional applied to fiction: if the character had, would have, could have
writing_prompts.example: One of my favourite stories is Romeo and Juliet by Shakespeare. The tragic ending of this play could have been avoided if several characters had made different decisions. If Friar Lawrence had sent his letter by a more reliable messenger, Romeo would have known that Juliet was not truly dead. If Romeo had not acted so quickly in despair, he would not have taken the poison. If only Juliet had woken up just a few minutes earlier, she would have seen Romeo alive and the entire tragedy would have been prevented. If their families had not been so consumed by their ancient feud, the two young lovers might have been able to marry openly and lived happily. If the play had ended differently, it would not have had the same powerful impact on audiences for over four hundred years, but it is fascinating to imagine what might have been.
